mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 3.5% signals a monetary easing cycle, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS below 3.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising GDPC1 above 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 2-month decline in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a weakening supply chain and demand for housing, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining INDPRO and HOUST are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals a monetary tightening cycle, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising CPIAUCSL above 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) and a 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE and declining PAYEMS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
